subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Swimming Particle — Direction Change, Acceleration & Distance
stimulus: Stephen swims back and forth along a straight path in a 50-meter-long pool for 90 seconds. Stephen's velocity is modeled by $v(t) = 2.38e^{-0.02t}\sin\left(\frac{\pi t}{56}\right)$, where $t$ is measured in seconds and $v(t)$ is measured in meters per second.

parts.prompt: Find all times $t$ in the interval $0 < t < 90$ at which Stephen changes direction. Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Considers sign of $v(t)$: sets $v(t) = 0$ and finds $t = 56$ in the interval $0 < t < 90$
1 pt: Stephen changes direction at $t = 56$ seconds because his velocity changes sign at that time
parts.scoring_notes: Must consider $v(t) = 0$ or the sign of $v(t)$ for first point. The only zero in $(0, 90)$ is $t = 56$ (since $\sin(\pi t/56) = 0 \Rightarrow t = 56$ in this interval). An answer of $t = 56$ with no supporting work earns no points.

parts.prompt: Find Stephen's acceleration at time $t = 60$ seconds. Show the setup for your calculations, and indicate units of measure. Is Stephen speeding up or slowing down at time $t = 60$ seconds? Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct acceleration with setup: $a(60) = v'(60) \approx -0.036$ meters per second per second
1 pt: Correct units: meters per second per second (or m/s$^2$)
1 pt: Stephen is speeding up at $t = 60$ seconds because $v(60) \approx -0.160 < 0$ and $a(60) \approx -0.036 < 0$ have the same sign
parts.scoring_notes: Must explicitly show $v'(60) = a(60)$ connection for setup point. Units are required. For the speed question: $v(60) < 0$ and $a(60) < 0$ (same sign) means speeding up. A response does not need to find the exact value of $v(60)$; stating $v(60) < 0$ (implied from $t = 60 > 56$) is sufficient.

parts.prompt: Find the distance between Stephen's position at time $t = 20$ seconds and his position at time $t = 80$ seconds. Show the setup for your calculations.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integral setup: $\left|\int_{20}^{80} v(t)\, dt\right|$ or equivalently $\left|x(80) - x(20)\right|$
parts.scoring_notes: The integral $\int_{20}^{80} v(t)\, dt$ gives displacement. The distance between positions is the absolute value. Answer $\approx 23.384$ meters. Only $\int_{20}^{80} v(t)\, dt$ earns the integral point (not $\int_{20}^{80} |v(t)|\, dt$ which would give total distance).

parts.prompt: Find the total distance Stephen swims over the time interval $0 \le t \le 90$ seconds. Show the setup for your calculations.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct integral setup: $\int_0^{90} |v(t)|\, dt$ or equivalent split: $\int_0^{56} v(t)\, dt - \int_{56}^{90} v(t)\, dt$
parts.scoring_notes: Total distance requires integrating $|v(t)|$ or splitting at $t = 56$. Answer $\approx 62.164$ meters. The answer point is earned for $62.164$ regardless of whether the setup point was earned.
